title: A New Hope: How CAR T-Cell Therapy Is Changing Cancer Treatment
subtitle: Scientists are using advanced technology to help the body's own immune system fight cancer more effectively.

summary.en: CAR T-cell therapy is a new medical treatment that helps the immune system find and destroy cancer cells. By adding a special 'GPS' to white blood cells, doctors can help them target specific diseases more accurately. While traditional treatments like chemotherapy are still very important, this new method offers hope for patients with blood cancers. Recent success stories, including that of actor Sam Neill, have brought more attention to this field. Researchers are now working to make this treatment work for more types of cancer in the future.
summary.zh: CAR T 細胞療法是一種新型醫療技術，能協助免疫系統發現並摧毀癌細胞。透過為白血球安裝特殊的「全球定位系統」，醫師能協助其更精準地鎖定特定疾病。雖然化學治療等傳統療法依然非常重要，但這種新方法為血液癌症病患帶來了希望。近期包括演員山姆尼爾在內的成功案例，已讓此領域受到更多關注。研究人員目前正致力於讓此療法在未來能應用於更多類型的癌症。

paragraphs.en: Modern medicine is reaching a new and exciting point in the fight against cancer. A treatment known as CAR T-cell therapy is gaining attention from scientists and the public alike. According to The Guardian Health, experts describe this method as a 'game-changer' because it uses the body’s own immune system to fight disease in a highly targeted way. Recently, the actor Sam Neill shared that his stage three cancer was in remission—meaning the signs of his disease have disappeared—after he received this treatment during a clinical trial in Sydney. He called the success 'science at its best.'
paragraphs.zh: 現代醫學在對抗癌症的道路上，正邁向一個嶄新且令人振奮的里程碑。一種稱為「嵌合抗原受體 T 細胞療法」（CAR T-cell therapy）的治療方式，正受到科學家與大眾的廣泛關注。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，專家將此療法譽為「改變遊戲規則」的技術，因為它能運用人體自身的免疫系統，以高度精準的方式對抗疾病。近期，演員山姆尼爾（Sam Neill）分享了他第三期癌症已進入緩解期的消息，意即疾病跡象已消失，而這正是他在雪梨參與一項臨床試驗並接受此療法後的成果。他稱這項成就為「科學最卓越的展現」。

paragraphs.en: To understand how this works, we must first look at the immune system. The body naturally defends itself against invaders like bacteria and viruses. It uses white blood cells, including T-cells, to identify and remove these threats. However, cancer cells are very clever; they often find ways to hide from the immune system so they can continue to grow. CAR T-cell therapy is designed to solve this problem. As explained by The Guardian Health, the process is like 'adding a GPS' to the T-cells. This helps the cells find their targets more easily.
paragraphs.zh: 要理解其運作原理，我們必須先探討免疫系統。人體會自然防禦細菌與病毒等外來入侵者，並利用包括 T 細胞在內的白血球來識別並清除這些威脅。然而，癌細胞非常狡猾，它們常能找到躲避免疫系統的方法，藉此持續生長。CAR T 細胞療法正是為了克服此問題而設計。正如《衛報健康》所解釋，這個過程就像是為 T 細胞「安裝全球定位系統」，能協助細胞更輕易地找到目標。

paragraphs.en: The process of creating this therapy is complex. First, doctors take T-cells from a patient’s blood. These cells are then changed in a laboratory using genetic engineering to give them the special 'GPS'—the chimeric antigen receptor. Once these enhanced cells are grown in the lab, they are put back into the patient’s body. Once inside, they multiply and begin to hunt down the cancer cells. This method is different from traditional treatments like chemotherapy. While chemotherapy uses strong medicine to kill cells throughout the body, CAR T-cell therapy is more specific. It targets only the proteins produced by the cancer, which can make it more effective for certain patients.
paragraphs.zh: 此療法的製作過程相當複雜。首先，醫師會從病患血液中提取 T 細胞，接著在實驗室中透過基因工程進行改造，賦予它們特殊的「全球定位系統」，即嵌合抗原受體。這些增強後的細胞在實驗室培養完成後，會被輸回病患體內。進入體內後，它們會開始增殖並獵殺癌細胞。此方法與化學治療等傳統療法不同；化學治療是使用強效藥物殺死全身細胞，而 CAR T 細胞療法則更具針對性，僅鎖定癌細胞產生的蛋白質，這使得它對特定病患更為有效。

paragraphs.en: There are also practical benefits to this new approach. According to The Guardian Health, the treatment is often faster than older methods. While chemotherapy can take months of regular hospital visits, CAR T-cell therapy usually requires only one infusion—a process where medicine is put into the blood through a tube—and a few weeks of care in the hospital. Additionally, one of the biggest advantages of this therapy is that the protective effect can last for a long time, which helps prevent the cancer from coming back, or relapsing.
paragraphs.zh: 這種新療法也具備實際優勢。根據《衛報健康》報導，此治療通常比舊有方法更迅速。化學治療可能需要數月定期往返醫院，而 CAR T 細胞療法通常只需一次輸注——即透過導管將藥物注入血液的過程——並在醫院接受幾週的照護即可。此外，此療法最大的優點之一在於其保護效果能持續很長一段時間，有助於預防癌症復發。

paragraphs.en: Despite these benefits, experts remind us that traditional treatments are not going away. Dr. Criselle D’Souza, an immunologist at the Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre, noted that chemotherapy remains a 'pillar' or a main support of cancer care. Chemotherapy is much cheaper, can treat a wider variety of cancers, and doctors have many years of experience in managing its side effects. Because of this, traditional treatments will continue to play a vital role for many patients for the foreseeable future.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管有這些優勢，專家仍提醒我們，傳統治療並不會消失。彼得麥卡勒姆癌症中心（Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre）的免疫學家克莉賽兒・德索薩（Criselle D’Souza）博士指出，化學治療依然是癌症照護的「基石」或主要支柱。化學治療成本低廉許多，能治療更多種類的癌症，且醫師在處理其副作用方面已有多年經驗。因此，在可預見的未來，傳統治療對許多病患而言仍將扮演至關重要的角色。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the future of CAR T-cell therapy is bright but still developing. Since 2018, four types of these therapies have been approved for use in Australia, but they are currently limited to blood cancers. Scientists are now working hard to adapt this technology to fight solid tumours, which are harder to target. While there are still challenges in making the cells find their targets in these different types of cancer, early results are promising. Prof Misty Jenkins from the Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research believes that we have only just begun to see what is possible. As research continues, the goal is to make this treatment more accessible and effective for a wider range of patients, potentially even turning it into a simpler injectable treatment one day.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，CAR T 細胞療法的前景光明，但仍在發展階段。自 2018 年以來，澳洲已核准四種此類療法，但目前僅限於治療血液癌症。科學家正致力於調整這項技術以對抗更難鎖定的實體腫瘤。儘管在讓細胞於這些不同類型的癌症中找到目標仍存在挑戰，但初步結果令人樂觀。沃爾特與伊麗莎霍爾醫學研究所（Walter and Eliza Hall Institute of Medical Research）的米斯蒂・詹金斯（Misty Jenkins）教授認為，我們才剛開始見證其潛力。隨著研究持續進行，目標是讓此療法對更多病患而言更易取得且更有效，甚至有朝一日能轉化為更簡單的注射治療。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An event, idea, or procedure that effects a significant shift in the current manner of doing or thinking about something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 改變遊戲規則的人事物；具有重大影響力的新事物。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞由 game（遊戲）與 changer（改變者）組成，在商業與科技報導中常用。文中：experts describe this method as a 'game-changer' because it uses the body’s own immune system to fight disease in a highly targeted way.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To search for someone or something with the intention of finding and capturing or destroying them.
keyTerms.noteZh: hunt 原意為狩獵，加上 down 強調追蹤到底的過程。文中：Once inside, they multiply and begin to hunt down the cancer cells.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A fundamental or essential part of a system or organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意為建築物的柱子，這裡引申為醫療體系中不可或缺的核心治療方式。文中：Dr. Criselle D’Souza, an immunologist at the Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre, noted that chemotherapy remains a 'pillar' or a main support of cancer care.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: The emerging cancer treatment that’s exciting scientists: ‘We’ve just scratched the surface on what’s possible’

quiz.question: According to the article, how does the process of CAR T-cell therapy differ from chemotherapy in terms of administration?

options.A: Chemotherapy involves genetic engineering of blood cells, while CAR T-cell therapy uses strong medicine.
options.B: CAR T-cell therapy typically requires only one infusion, whereas chemotherapy often involves months of hospital visits.
options.C: Chemotherapy is used exclusively for solid tumors, while CAR T-cell therapy is used for blood cancers.
options.D: CAR T-cell therapy is a cheaper, more accessible alternative that replaces the need for hospital stays.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第四段明確指出，CAR T-cell 療法通常只需一次輸液（infusion）及幾週的住院照護，而化療則可能需要數月的定期醫院回診。選項 A 敘述錯誤，因為基因工程是 CAR T-cell 療法的步驟；選項 C 雖然提到兩者差異，但文中並未說化療僅用於實體腫瘤；選項 D 錯誤，因為第五段提到化療其實比 CAR T-cell 療法更便宜。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might CAR T-cell therapy be considered a 'game-changer' despite its current limitations?

options.A: It has already replaced chemotherapy as the primary treatment for all types of cancer.
options.B: It is significantly cheaper to produce than any other existing cancer treatment.
options.C: It offers a highly targeted approach that can potentially prevent cancer from returning.
options.D: It allows patients to avoid all side effects associated with traditional cancer treatments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段與第四段，CAR T-cell 療法透過改造免疫細胞來精準鎖定癌細胞，且具有長效保護力，能預防癌症復發，這使得它成為革命性的療法。選項 A 錯誤，因為第五段提到化療仍是重要支柱；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到化療更便宜；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提及該療法完全沒有副作用。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'pillar' mean in the context of Dr. D’Souza’s description of chemotherapy?

options.A: A physical structure used to support hospital equipment.
options.B: A fundamental and essential component of a system.
options.C: A medical device used to administer infusions.
options.D: A temporary solution that will soon be replaced.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，'pillar'（支柱）被用來比喻化療在癌症照護體系中扮演不可或缺、基礎且核心的角色。選項 A 是字面上的物理意義，不符合語意；選項 C 與醫療設備無關；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到化療在可預見的未來仍將持續扮演重要角色，而非即將被取代的暫時方案。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the state of cancer treatment?

options.A: CAR T-cell therapy is a promising, targeted technology that complements traditional treatments while continuing to evolve.
options.B: Traditional treatments like chemotherapy are becoming obsolete due to the rapid success of genetic engineering.
options.C: The main goal of modern medicine is to eliminate all hospital visits by moving toward injectable treatments.
options.D: Scientists have successfully solved the problem of targeting solid tumors using CAR T-cell therapy.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文章整體論述了 CAR T-cell 療法的創新性與優勢，同時透過專家觀點強調它與傳統療法（如化療）的互補關係，並提到該技術仍在發展中。選項 B 與文章觀點衝突；選項 C 過度推論，文中僅提到未來可能簡化；選項 D 錯誤，因為第六段明確指出目前針對實體腫瘤的應用仍面臨挑戰，尚在研究中。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
